title: React Higher-Order Components (HOCs) Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of HOC pattern, reusability, and naming conventions.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primary role of a Higher-Order Component (HOC) in React?
options: To speed up the app by caching components.
To reuse component logic across multiple components.
To replace the standard React hooks.
To provide a replacement for CSS styles.

questions.question: An HOC is a ________ that takes a ________ and returns a ________.
options: Component, functional component, JSX element.
Function, component, new component.
Hook, state, updated state.
Class, method, instance.

questions.question: Which of the following is an industry-standard naming convention for HOCs?
options: withName
makeName
getProps
wrapName

questions.question: Can an HOC add additional props to the component it wraps?
options: Yes, this is one of their main use cases.
No, they can only modify state.
Yes, but only in older versions of React.
None of the above.

questions.question: Will an HOC change the original component it wraps?
options: Yes, it modifies the original component.
No, it returns a new component that wraps the original one.
Yes, but only in class-based syntax.
Only if you return it with an ID.
